title: Stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq, Dow futures rise as US and Iran reportedly call a halt to attacks
summary: Stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq, Dow futures rise as US and Iran reportedly call a halt to attacks
extracted_text: US stock futures gained on Monday following reports that the US and Iran have agreed to stop tit-for-tat attacks that broke out over the weekend, enabling peace talks to continue. Nasdaq 100 (NQ=F) futures rose 0.8%, while those on the S&P 500 (ES=F) moved up 0.5%. Contracts on the Dow Jones Industrial Average (YM=F) put on 0.2%. Markets headed into a holiday-shortened week on edge after the US launched strikes on Iranian military targets over the weekend. Oil prices moved higher: Brent crude futures gained 0.7% to around $73 a barrel, while US West Texas Intermediate futures rose 1% to nearly $70. On Friday, the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite booked weekly losses of over 2% and 4.5%, respectively. 'Magnificent Seven' big techs Nvidia and Alphabet both saw shares slide over 8%. But the Dow eked out a 0.6% gain as healthcare stocks provided support. The highlight of the coming week will be the June jobs report, due Thursday (pulled forward from Friday due to Independence Day).

title: Stock market next week: Outlook for June 29-July 3, 2026
summary: Stock market next week: Outlook for June 29-July 3, 2026
extracted_text: JPMorgan raised its 2026 S&P 500 target to 7,800 (from 7,200), implying ~5% further upside under a 'Blue Sky' scenario. Thursday's PCE report showed inflation at its highest in roughly three years, but consumer spending remains robust. Holiday-shortened week (July 4th weekend) means lower trading volumes and wilder market swings. End-of-month/quarter rebalancing by portfolio managers could add additional volatility. BofA technical strategist Paul Ciana warned that correction risks are moving higher and advised clients to take on protection heading into Q3. Hyperscalers: some are reducing exposure until there's more confidence in sustained extraordinary earnings growth. Semiconductors (especially memory): remain the biggest AI beneficiary but many fear they have run up too far. Fed funds futures now price a rate hike as soon as September. Cronk's preferences: US over international, large- and mid-cap over small-cap, best value in financials and industrials.

title: Oil price: renewed U.S.-Iran strikes reignite Middle East supply fears
summary: Oil price: renewed U.S.-Iran strikes reignite Middle East supply fears
extracted_text: Oil prices traded mixed after fresh US-Iran military strikes revived concerns over Middle East crude supplies. WTI Crude (Aug '26) at $69.52/bbl (+0.4%), Brent Crude at $71.90/bbl (-0.1%). The US military struck 10 Iranian targets in and near the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday in retaliation for a drone strike on the Panamanian-flagged tanker M/T Kiku, which was carrying over 2 million barrels of crude oil. President Trump posted on Truth Social: 'There may come a point when we are no longer able to be reasonable, and will be forced to militarily complete the job that we very successfully started. If that happens, the Islamic Republic of Iran will no longer exist!' ING strategists Warren Patterson and Ewa Manthey: 'This complacency is odd and clearly leaves significant upside risk if the supply recovery proves slow – or if we see significant re-escalation.'

title: US Market Outlook: Jobs Data Steers the Week Ahead
summary: US Market Outlook: Jobs Data Steers the Week Ahead
extracted_text: June NFP lands Thursday, July 2 (pulled forward due to Independence Day). Consensus expects ~170,000 new jobs, a slowdown from May. A hotter-than-expected print could reinforce the hawkish Fed narrative. Core PCE inflation rose to 3.4% year-on-year in May (highest since October 2023). Fed raised its 2026 core inflation projection from 2.7% to 3.3%; does not see 2% until 2028 at the earliest. Nike (NKE) reports Tuesday after close: consensus revenue ~$10.85 billion (-3% YoY), EPS ~$0.12. CEO Elliott Hill labels this quarter as the 'low point of the Win Now turnaround.' Nvidia (NVDA) and other AI leaders have driven the Nasdaq up ~25% in two months. The rally is narrow — a handful of mega-caps doing the heavy lifting.

title: Oil Price Today, June 29, 2026: Crude Oil Climbs as U.S.-Iran Trade Blows Despite Ceasefire; Brent Tops $72
summary: Oil Price Today, June 29, 2026: Crude Oil Climbs as U.S.-Iran Trade Blows Despite Ceasefire; Brent Tops $72
extracted_text: Brent crude futures gained 0.7% to $72.5 a barrel, while US WTI crude advanced 1.2% to $70 a barrel. Monday's recovery reflects fresh concerns that the conflict could once again disrupt oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz, a strategic waterway that handles roughly a fifth of global oil shipments. The latest gains follow several days of tit-for-tat strikes between Washington and Tehran. Prior to Monday's gain, both benchmarks had posted steep weekly losses, with Brent tumbling more than 10% for its third consecutive weekly decline.

title: Fundamental Market Analysis for June 29, 2026 (EURUSD, GBPUSD, USDJPY)
summary: Fundamental Market Analysis for June 29, 2026 (EURUSD, GBPUSD, USDJPY)
extracted_text: EUR/USD: ECB raised rates by 25 bps but lowered 2026 euro area growth forecast to 0.8%. US Fed maintained target range at 3.50%-3.75%. Divergence points to further EUR/USD downside. GBP/USD: BOE kept rate at 3.75% (two MPC members voted for hike). UK inflation eased to 2.8%. Political uncertainty after UK leadership change weighs on sterling. USD/JPY: BOJ raised short-term policy rate to ~1.0% but rate gap with US remains substantial. Pair near multi-year highs with proximity to 162 increasing likelihood of official Japanese intervention. Japanese authorities have repeatedly signalled readiness to counteract excessive currency moves.

summary: Analyst Upgrades and Downgrades for Monday, 6/29

title: Earnings Calendar and Analysis for This Week (June 29-July 3)
summary: Earnings Calendar and Analysis for This Week (June 29-July 3)
extracted_text: Nike (NKE) reports fiscal Q4 results after Tuesday's close. Consensus: EPS $0.13 (-7.1% YoY), Revenue $10.85 billion (-2.2% YoY). NKE down >33% YTD through June 26. Analyst ratings: 12 Buy/Strong Buy, 24 Hold, 2 Sell/Strong Sell. Needham's Tom Nikic (Hold): 'We remain cautious on NKE shares, as we believe that the pace of the turnaround remains frustratingly slow.' Constellation Brands (STZ) reports Tuesday after close: EPS estimate $3.23. General Mills (GIS) reports Wednesday before open: EPS estimate $0.80. Friday July 3: US stock and bond markets closed (Independence Day).

title: Nike Earnings Preview: NKE Stock Tests $40 Support as Turnaround Doubts Mount
summary: Nike Earnings Preview: NKE Stock Tests $40 Support as Turnaround Doubts Mount
extracted_text: Nike (NKE) closed at $40.75 on June 26, near lowest levels in over a decade. Reports fiscal Q4 2026 after Tuesday close. Revenue expectation ~$10.8B, roughly 2% YoY decline. EPS expectation $0.11-$0.13. Shares fallen more than 35% YTD. Deutsche Bank cut target to $43 from $51 (Hold). Oppenheimer cut to $60 from $120, warns 2026 could be restructuring year. Critical technical support at $40; breakdown could target $35. Resistance cluster at $43-44 zone must be reclaimed for recovery attempt. RSI at 30.09 near oversold. China weakness, inventory glut remain key concerns.

title: Micron Stock Jumps After Blowout Earnings: Buy or Wait?
summary: Micron Stock Jumps After Blowout Earnings: Buy or Wait?
extracted_text: Micron Q3 FY2026 (reported June 24): Revenue of $41.46 billion came in roughly $6 billion ahead of consensus; adjusted EPS of $25.11 topped estimates by more than $4; Q4 revenue guidance of approximately $50 billion was materially above Wall Street consensus. Adjusted gross margin: 84.9%. 16 multi-year Strategic Customer Agreements covering ~20% of DRAM volume and up to 1/3 of NAND, with ~$100B in aggregate revenue obligations disclosed. Data-center revenue >$25B in Q3. Enterprise SSD revenue more than doubled sequentially. HBM4 volume shipments began for Nvidia Vera Rubin platform; 2026 supply fully contracted. Goldman Sachs: DRAM supply-demand conditions tightest in 15 years. Pre-market June 29: $1,139.39 (+0.62%). Phillip Securities raised PT to $1,870 from $530.

title: Week Ahead: 29 June 2026
summary: Week Ahead: 29 June 2026
extracted_text: Russell 2000 (small-cap) YTD: ~+21%. Nasdaq 100 YTD: ~+17%. S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 set for weekly losses; Russell 2000 and Dow Jones heading higher. Q2 earnings season three weeks away: analysts expect ~22% earnings growth. US core PCE: +0.3% MoM in May; annual rate 3.4% (highest since Oct 2023). US Q1 GDP final revision: 2.1% annualised. WTI crude: -5.55% to $71.65. DXY: +0.73% to 101.49. Bitcoin: -5.72% to $59,628. Gold: -3.4% to $4,014. VIX: rose to 18.88 from 16.41. Key events: China NBS manufacturing PMI Tuesday (consensus 50.1), Euro Area flash inflation Wednesday, US NFP Thursday.

summary: China, Hong Kong stocks rise, led by consumer, healthcare shares

title: S&P 500 Record High 2026 — The Tech Marketer
summary: S&P 500 Record High 2026 — The Tech Marketer
extracted_text: The S&P 500 closed at 7,609.78 on June 2, 2026, its first close above 7,600 in history. The index reached an intraday high of 7,620.90, a new all-time record, before settling at the closing level. Historical context: index has pulled back since then, closing at 7,354.02 on June 26.

title: Small Cap Rotation 2026 — Russell 2000 +21% YTD
summary: Small Cap Rotation 2026 — Russell 2000 +21% YTD
extracted_text: The Russell 2000 hit an all-time high of 2,888.62 on May 6, 2026. The iShares Russell 2000 ETF (IWM) is up 31% over the past 12 months. That is the longest small-cap outperformance streak since 1996. The Russell 2000 is up 15.6% in 2026 (earlier data), ahead of the S&P 500's 9.2% gain, the Nasdaq's 13.3% advance, and the Dow's 5.2% rise. Small-cap value outperforming growth within the small-cap universe.
